formal_event: Prospectus states that the company does not undertake government financing functions and that new debt after January 1 2015 is not local government debt.
post_event_function: Formal no-government-financing language coexists with a major reorganization of public-project finance. The issuer remains a leading Fuzhou infrastructure, shantytown-renovation, affordable-housing, municipal代建, and area-development platform, but the prospectus documents that after 2011 municipal project funding shifted from issuer financing with fiscal repayment to full fiscal appropriation and management-fee settlement. PPP projects, government-purchase-service projects that were later adjusted, fiscal subsidies, government receivables, and rating evidence of continuing fiscal support all point to functional transfer rather than substantive disappearance.

formal_event: Legal opinion reports that Fuzhou SASAC approved the company name change from Fuzhou State-Owned Assets Investment Development Group to Fuzhou State Capital Investment and Operation Group in January 2022 and changed its business scope from urban infrastructure, area development, utilities, strategic-industry development, and real estate to headquarters management, own-fund investment, venture investment, and consulting.
post_event_function: The same legal issuer was converted from a development-oriented state-owned assets investment group into a state-capital investment and operation platform. Direct urban infrastructure and area-development language disappeared from the issuer business scope, while municipal infrastructure functions continued through equity stakes in Fuzhou Chengtou, Fuzhou Zuohai, and other city platforms and through the Mindu infrastructure fund. This is a transfer into portfolio and fund channels rather than substantive disappearance.

post_event_function: The 2023 restructuring created an industrial-investment holding group and contains formal government-financing-function stripping language. But the same group system continues to show traffic infrastructure construction, land整理, fiscal-payment arrangements, government-linked receivables, and repurchase-payment risk. The corporate label changed more clearly than the quasi-fiscal development function, so this is coded as nominal exit.
